children.text: developing and enhancing effective school library programs, which may include providing professional development for school librarians, books, and up-to-date materials to high-need schools;

early literacy services, including pediatric literacy programs through which, during well-child visits, medical providers trained in research-based methods of early language and literacy promotion provide developmentally appropriate books and recommendations to parents to encourage them to read aloud to their children starting in infancy; and
programs that provide high-quality books on a regular basis to children and adolescents from low-income communities to increase reading motivation, performance, and frequency.

children.label: A
children.text: a local educational agency in which 20 percent or more of the students served by the local educational agency are from families with an income below the poverty line;

children.label: B
children.text: a consortium of such local educational agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: the Bureau of Indian Education; or

children.label: A
children.text: is supported by staff, which may include volunteers, or affiliates at the State and local levels; and

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrates effectiveness or high-quality plans for addressing childhood literacy activities for the population targeted by the grant.
